title: Single-binary packaging — .beam file embedding
purpose: Meridian agents should deploy as a single self-contained binary with no external .beam file dependencies. This brief implements the .beam embedding path (the first of two modes per D10): a build-time tool embeds .beam files into the beamr binary as a static asset, and the loader checks the embedded archive before the filesystem. This is independent of the JIT pipeline — embed.rs lives in the loader module, not in jit/. Native code embedding follows in a separate brief when AOT is ready.
task: Implement .beam file embedding in crates/beamr/src/loader/embed.rs. A build script (build.rs) or proc-macro reads .beam files from a configured directory, compresses them, and embeds them as a static byte array via include_bytes!. At runtime, the loader's module resolution checks the embedded archive first, then falls back to the filesystem. Embedded modules are discoverable via the same module_info/code_info paths as filesystem-loaded modules.

requirements.title: Embedded archive format
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an EmbeddedArchive format consisting of: a magic number header (b"BEAMR_EMBED\0"), a version byte (1), a module count (u32 LE), followed by a sequence of (module_name_len: u16 LE, module_name: [u8], beam_data_len: u32 LE, beam_data: [u8]) entries. THE SYSTEM SHALL compress each beam_data entry with zstd at build time. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use a custom binary format for individual .beam files — they are stored as-is (compressed) and decompressed on load.
acceptance: EmbeddedArchive::parse(bytes) -> Result<EmbeddedArchive, EmbedError> validates magic + version
EmbeddedArchive::modules() -> impl Iterator<Item = (&str, &[u8])> yields (name, compressed_beam) pairs
EmbeddedArchive::get(module_name) -> Option<Vec<u8>> decompresses and returns the .beam bytes
Invalid magic or version returns EmbedError::InvalidArchive
Test: round-trip — pack 3 modules, parse, get each -> matches original bytes
Test: parse empty bytes -> InvalidArchive

requirements.title: Build-time archive packer
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an archive_pack function (usable from build.rs) that reads .beam files from a directory, compresses each with zstd, and writes the EmbeddedArchive format to a byte vector. THE SYSTEM SHALL sort modules alphabetically for deterministic output. THE SYSTEM SHALL skip non-.beam files in the directory. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT fail if the directory is empty — produce a valid archive with zero modules.
acceptance: archive_pack(beam_dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<u8>, EmbedError> reads and packs all .beam files
Output starts with magic header and correct module count
Each module's data is zstd-compressed
Modules are sorted alphabetically by name
Non-.beam files are silently skipped
Empty directory produces a valid archive with count=0
Test: pack directory with 3 .beam files -> archive_parse yields 3 modules in sorted order

requirements.title: Loader integration — embedded archive as first resolution source
requirements.spec: WHEN resolving a module load request, THE SYSTEM SHALL check the embedded archive before the filesystem. WHEN the module is found in the embedded archive, THE SYSTEM SHALL decompress and load it as if it were read from the filesystem. WHEN the module is not in the embedded archive, THE SYSTEM SHALL fall through to filesystem resolution (existing behaviour unchanged). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT embed modules when no archive is compiled in — the embedded archive is an optional static asset (empty archive by default).
acceptance: Module resolution order: embedded archive -> filesystem code paths
Embedded module loads successfully via the standard load_module path
Non-embedded module falls through to filesystem with no error
When no archive is compiled in: all loads go to filesystem (no overhead beyond a single Option check)
Test: embed module 'foo', load_module('foo') succeeds without foo.beam on disk
Test: load_module('bar') with bar.beam on disk but not embedded succeeds via filesystem

requirements.title: Embedded module discoverability via module_info
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL make embedded modules discoverable through the same module_info and code:all_loaded introspection paths as filesystem-loaded modules. WHEN an embedded module is loaded, THE SYSTEM SHALL record its origin as embedded (not filesystem) in the module registry metadata. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a way to list all embedded module names without loading them.
acceptance: ModuleOrigin enum has variants: Filesystem(PathBuf), Embedded, Preloaded
Loaded embedded modules have origin = Embedded in module registry
module_info(module, source) returns embedded for embedded modules
EmbeddedArchive::module_names() -> Vec<&str> lists all embedded module names
Test: load embedded module, query module_info -> origin is Embedded
Test: module_names() returns sorted list matching packed modules

requirements.title: Static embedding via include_bytes!
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a mechanism to embed the archive at compile time using include_bytes! referencing a build-generated archive file. The build.rs script packs the archive from a configurable BEAMR_EMBED_DIR environment variable. WHEN BEAMR_EMBED_DIR is not set, THE SYSTEM SHALL compile with an empty embedded archive. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require BEAMR_EMBED_DIR for a successful build — embedding is opt-in.
acceptance: build.rs reads BEAMR_EMBED_DIR env var, packs archive, writes to OUT_DIR/embedded_archive.bin
embed.rs uses include_bytes!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/embedded_archive.bin")) for the static data
When BEAMR_EMBED_DIR is set: archive contains .beam files from that directory
When BEAMR_EMBED_DIR is not set: archive is empty (magic + version + count=0)
cargo build without BEAMR_EMBED_DIR succeeds (no error, empty archive)
Test: BEAMR_EMBED_DIR=/path/to/beams cargo build embeds the modules

boundaries: SHALL NOT embed native code (AOT-compiled) in this brief — native embedding follows when AOT is ready
SHALL NOT implement hot code loading for embedded modules — embedded modules are immutable (load once, no reload)
SHALL NOT implement archive encryption or signing — plaintext compressed .beam only (zstd is an always-on dependency for embed decompression)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
embed.rs is under 500 lines of production code
cargo build without BEAMR_EMBED_DIR succeeds with no warnings
Embedded module round-trips: pack -> embed -> load -> execute -> correct result
